characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The last known "Yellow One" to exist in all of Pac-World, the young Pac possesses abilities far and beyond the rest of his kind, which he uses in superhero fashion to protect Pac-Land and the rest of the world from the ongoing attacks from the netherworld. Adaptation Name Change: He often goes by Pacster or Pac, instead Pac-Man (though the latter is still used). Affectionate Nickname: His friends (mostlySpiral) refer to him as Pacster from time to time. Badass Adorable: A cheerful and goofy kid who eventually can take on the most dangerous forces in his universe. Beware the Silly Ones: Pac is a glutton with his head in the clouds and an almost naive temperament. Next thing you know, on a moment's notice, he's raining fire from the sky, pile-driving monsters in a steel body, freezing over an evil army, or taking out an apocalyptic threat by becoming a planet—all on a moment's notice. If that's a bit much to read, just note that theentirety of the Netherworld, after being free for the first time in years, ran backscreaming in fearupon justseeinghim.

characters.name: Pac-Man
characters.description: The last known "Yellow One" to exist in all of Pac-World, the young Pac possesses abilities far and beyond the rest of his kind, which he uses in superhero fashion to protect Pac-Land and the rest of the world from the ongoing attacks from the netherworld. Adaptation Name Change: He often goes by Pacster or Pac, instead Pac-Man (though the latter is still used). Affectionate Nickname: His friends (mostlySpiral) refer to him as Pacster from time to time. Badass Adorable: A cheerful and goofy kid who eventually can take on the most dangerous forces in his universe. Beware the Silly Ones: Pac is a glutton with his head in the clouds and an almost naive temperament. Next thing you know, on a moment's notice, he's raining fire from the sky, pile-driving monsters in a steel body, freezing over an evil army, or taking out an apocalyptic threat by becoming a planet—all on a moment's notice. If that's a bit much to read, just note that theentirety of the Netherworld, after being free for the first time in years, ran backscreaming in fearupon justseeinghim.

characters.name: Cylindria
characters.description: Action Girl: While she needs a hoverboard and ghost sucking gun, she can fend off ghosts just as well as Spiral can. Affectionate Nickname: She goes by "Cyli" to her friends, despite the pun (pronounced "Silly"). Betty and Veronica: The Betty to Pac's Archie and Pinky's Veronica. Canon Foreigner: She was created for the show. Curtains Match the Windows: In the sense that her eyes are pink like her the streaks in her hair.

characters.name: Spiral
characters.description: Badass Normal: Unlike Pacster, he can’t eat ghosts, but he has a ghost sucking gun that helps him grab them. He also manages to survive many situations despite having very abilities at his disposal. Big Brother Instinct: He's very protective of his best friend Pac. Big Eater: In "All You Can Eat", Spiral is shown to have a huge appetite, and he claims that he’s thesecond-biggest eater in all of Pac-World, next to Pac. The Big Guy:Subverted Trope. Spiral is the biggest of the main three, but, by the end of the first episode, it’s made clear that Pac-Man is most powerful of the three in physical combat. That being said, it’s implied that hewasstronger before Pac-Man unlocked his full potential. Canon Foreigner: He was created for the show.

characters.name: Sir Cumference
characters.description: Canon Foreigner: He was created for the show. Cool Old Guy: A wacky but quite wise old scientist. December–December Romance: With Pac's Aunt Spheria. Expy: Bears a striking resemblance toDr. Elefun. Long-Lived: Is somehow still alive and kicking in "Rip Van Packle", even though it takes place 70 years later and he wasalreadyan elderly man in the present era.Of course, as it turns out the episode was only a dream, he might've only lived that long because Pac needed someone like him to find a solution to end hisBad Futurenightmare.

characters.name: President Stratos Spheros
characters.description: Alliterative Name:StratosSpheros. Big Good: While a bit arrogant and shifty, he is still a pretty good guy and is definitely a more benevolent ruler than his brother. Butt-Monkey: Takes a lot of abuse from both villains and collateral damage during Pac-Man's fights. Canon Foreigner: He was created for the show. Cool Old Guy: Albeit not as much as Sir Cumference.

characters.name: Spheria Suprema
characters.description: The Ace: She's so skilled at Pac-Pong that the only other player that's been able to challenge her was Betrayus. Alliterative Name:SpheriaSuprema. Doubles as aRhyming Name. Canon Foreigner: She was created for the show. Cool Aunt: Probably the person Pac-Man can rely on the most. Cool Old Lady: She may be retired, but she can still pack a punch and has a noble heart.

characters.name: Lord Betrayus
characters.description: 0% Approval Rating: With the exception of Fluffy (but even then he has his limits with him), despite being the ruler of the Netherworld, nobody likes Betrayus. Not only has he been mocked several times in the series, his own minions seem to follow him out of fear or to get their bodies back while Pac-World hates him forunderstandable reasons. He even lampshades in "The Shadow of the Were-Pac" that nobody likes him. Amazon Chaser: He's got a thing for Spheria Suprema, aretired soldierwho previously defeated him in a match of Pac-pong. Artificial Limbs: Since ghosts don't have actual hands, Betrayus augments this by wearing belted gloves withsharp metal fingers. Bad Boss: Betrayus is a jerk to almost everybody, and his minions are no exception. Big Bad: The main villain of the series, who commands all and answers to none. However, it's been implied thatsomeone or something is on a higher level than he.

characters.name: Butt-ler
characters.description: Affably Evil: He's genuinely polite to everyone, due to being a butler. Pac Man takes the time to talk to him when he tests out his brother inventions out on the field. Because You Were Nice to Me:At the end of "The Nerd Is The Word", he tells Pacster what Betrayus will do to him after that Pac and friends spent the entire episode treating him better than Betrayus ever did. Butt-Monkey: Poor old Butt-ler never gets a break. Canon Foreigner: He was created for the show. Embarrassing Last Name: He was known as CorporalHeineyhead, which would frequently make his last name the "butt" of many jokes...

characters.name: Dr. Buttocks
characters.description: Artificial Limbs: Dr. Buttocks' right arm is a pincer. Beware the Silly Ones: While he may be aButt-Monkeyand stereotypical mad scientist, he also nearly wiped out all life on the planet via Ghosteroid, which he was able to summon through a scheme no more difficult than any other he's ever prepared. Blind Without 'Em: He can't see if he loses his glasses. Book Smart: As befitting of aMad Scientist, according to him, he was at the top of his class at Pac-World U which suggests he's great academically. Canon Foreigner: He was created for the show.

characters.name: The Ghost Gang
characters.description: Adaptational Heroism: In comparison to how they were initially antagonists in the original continuity, they’re allies of Pac-People in this continuity. Beauty Equals Goodness: They're a little less scary-looking than the average ghost (most notably, they're missing thejagged mouthsmost of their brethren have). Big, Thin, Short Trio: Out of the males members, Clyde is the heftiest of the group, Inky is the lankiest group, and Blinky is the shortest group. Composite Character:Blinky is a mixture of the two Clydes from both the Hanna-Babara cartoon and the "World" series. (voice tone and attitude).Pinky is also a mix of both Sue from the Hanna-Babara cartoon (snarky attitude) and Pinky from the "World" series (having a crush on the main hero).Clyde here takes the design of hisPac-Man Partyincarnation and the cross-eyes that Inky had. Blinky is a mixture of the two Clydes from both the Hanna-Babara cartoon and the "World" series. (voice tone and attitude).

characters.name: The Pointy Heads
characters.description: Alien Animals: The Pointy Heads are cartoonish squids, but gray and with pointy heads. Alien Invasion: "Invasion of the Pointy Heads". They even team up and backstab Betrayus and the rest of the ghosts. Canon Foreigner: They were created for the show. Expies: Of the aliens from the classicSpace Invadersarcade game. Humongous Mecha: They use giant cyclop mecha.

characters.name: Obtuse
characters.description: Pac-World's chief crime lord. He has worked for Betrayus on at least two occasions. Canon Foreigner: He was created for the show. President Evil: He manages to get himself elected as President (with Betrayus' help) in "Peace Without Slime" and "The Ghost Behind the Throne." His first order is to establish "peace" with the ghosts, by allowing them into Pac-World. The Quisling: He sides with Betrayus, helping him and his ghost army to take over Pac-World. Villain with Good Publicity: Obtuse being a crime lord seems to be a very well-kept secret, as no other Pac-Worlder is aware of his criminal ways. He even manages to make a successful run for President with no references to any criminal activities being brought up by the general public.

characters.name: Elliptika
characters.description: Spheros and Betrayus' niece through their sister, who resides in Pac-Tokyo. She comes to visit Pacopolis in "New Girl in Town." Betty and Veronica: Had the series continued, she would have fulfilled this role to Pinky in Cyli’s place. Canon Foreigner: She was created for the show. Gratuitous Japanese: In English versions. It was averted in Japanese versions though. Last Episode, New Character: Her debut episode also happens to be the last episode before the series' cancellation. In a non-cancellation and Season 2-only sense, she also happens to be introduced in the finale.

characters.name: Skeebo
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer: Towards Cylindria. He used to be her boyfriend, but when she was grabbed by a huge ghost inThe Pilot,he dumped her so that he wouldn’t have to save her. In "Heebo-Skeebo" and "Stand By Your Pac-Man", he tries to win her back, but she wants nothing to do with him after seeing what a selfish, cowardly jerk he is. Arbitrary Skepticism: In "A Berry Scary Night", Skeebo claims that vampacs don’t exist. Pac reminds Skeebo that he used to say that aboutghosts, too. Sure enough, Skeebo is proven wrong again when Count Pacula comes to Pacopolis. Attention Whore: In "Heebo-Skeebo", he tries to become a superhero so he can become famous like Pac. In "Robo Woes", he wants to sing with Pac-Man and his friends at the President’s party. Bring My Brown Pants: In "Meanie Genie", when he gets frightened by ghosts, for a split second apuddle appears beneath him. The Bully: He bullies Pac for being yellow.
